content.problemStatement:
Chef has a **binary** array AAA of length NNN. In one operation, Chef does the following:

**1.** Select any LLL and RRR such that (1вЙ§L<RвЙ§вИ£AвИ£)(1 \le L \lt R \le |A|)(1вЙ§L<RвЙ§вИ£AвИ£) \\
**2.** Add ALвКХAL+1вКХвА¶вКХARA_L \oplus A_{L+1} \oplus \ldots \oplus A_RALвАЛвКХAL+1вАЛвКХвА¶вКХARвАЛ to his score (Here, вКХ\oplusвКХ denotes the bitwise XOR operation) \\
**3.** Remove the subarray ALвА¶RA_{L \dots R}ALвА¶RвАЛ from AAA

Determine the **maximum** score Chef can get after performing the above operation any number of times.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT вАФ the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN вАФ the size of the array AAA.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,вА¶,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1вАЛ,A2вАЛ,вА¶,ANвАЛ denoting the array AAA.


Output Format
For each test case, output the maximum score Chef can get.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2вЛЕ1052 \cdot 10^52вЛЕ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1: We can perform the following moves:

A=[1,0,0,0,1]A = [1, 0, 0, 0, 1]A=[1,0,0,0,1]. Select L=1L = 1L=1 and R=3R = 3R=3 and remove subarray [1,0,0][1, 0, 0][1,0,0]. AAA becomes [0,1][0, 1][0,1].
A=[0,1]A = [0, 1]A=[0,1]. Select L=1L = 1L=1 and R=2R = 2R=2 and remove subarray [0,1][0, 1][0,1]. AAA becomes [][][].

Total score =1+1=2= 1 + 1 = 2=1+1=2
Test Case 2: We can perform the following move:

A=[1,1,1]A = [1, 1, 1]A=[1,1,1]. Select L=1L = 1L=1 and R=3R = 3R=3 and remove subarray [1,1,1][1, 1, 1][1,1,1]. AAA becomes [][][].

Total score =1= 1=1
